Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephropathy and tubular dysfunction. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia, but transient glucosuria may be present. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
